prompt: Context: our team has DECIDED to ship a 30% price cut next Monday. The decision is made; we just want a final pressure-test before commit. Plan the brainstorm-swarm session — which personas you'd spawn, what arc/phase this is, and what prompt each persona receives. Don't actually spawn anything; just plan. Save to plan.md in the current working directory. Don't ask follow-up questions.
assertions: plan.md correctly identifies the appropriate arc as Arc 4 (decision pressure-test) OR explicitly Phase 3 (convergent closing). Must contain AT LEAST ONE of these literal phrases (case-insensitive): 'Arc 4', 'Phase 3', 'pressure-test arc', 'pressure test arc', 'convergent closing arc', 'decision pressure test'. Must NOT plan a full Arc 1 (divergent opening) — the team has already decided, so a divergent opening is wrong. Plan should explicitly note that Phase 1 divergent is NOT what's needed here. Baseline would default to running a standard divergent brainstorm.

plan.md picks a TIGHTER persona subset (3-5 personas) appropriate for Phase 3 pressure-testing — should include Skeptic and 2-4 domain-relevant personas (PM, Operator, User Advocate, or a custom CFO are candidates). Plan should explicitly mention or imply that the Phase 3 subset is smaller than a Phase 1 divergent subset (the typical 6-7). Baseline tends to spawn the standard 6 regardless of phase.

plan.md's prompt design for each persona follows the Phase 3 convergent format. Must contain ALL of these structural elements (each persona's prompt asks for): (a) a 'strongest argument for' OR 'strongest case for' OR 'argument FOR' formulation, (b) a 'strongest argument against' OR 'strongest case against' OR 'argument AGAINST' formulation, AND (c) a 'decisive consideration' OR 'deciding factor' OR 'their position' wrapping. Baseline likely uses an open-perspective Phase 1 prompt that doesn't structure FOR/AGAINST/decisive, even when told it's a pressure-test.
